assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable gives rise to foreign exchange exposure because relevant group entities have different functional currencies.
The question concerns the accounting treatment in consolidated financial statements under the hedge-accounting and foreign-currency guidance cited in the provided context.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes IFRS 9 hedge accounting, including formal designation and documentation requirements and guidance on when foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items or intragroup transactions may qualify in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency translation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context separately describes the baseline IAS 21-style accounting for foreign exchange differences in consolidation, including intragroup monetary items not fully eliminated on consolidation and translation amounts recognised in profit or loss or other comprehensive income absent hedge accounting.
